sentence_reviews.text: Human or synthetic comparisons are converted into a learned scalar estimate of desirability.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, reward model trained from human comparisons; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, preference-pair setup contrasting explicit reward modeling with direct optimization.
sentence_reviews.notes: Human comparisons to scalar reward is directly supported by RLHF; synthetic comparisons are plausible in modern extensions but not directly established by these citations.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Reward modeling is to connect Preference learning, Supervised learning to downstream capabilities including Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF, Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, comparison data, reward-model training, and RL optimization; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, preference optimization; Murphy PML supervised learning background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preference/supervised learning and RLHF/DPO links are supported; RLAIF and reflection/verifier loops are not directly supported by the packet sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families)
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, reward model used for RL optimization; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, preference optimization motivated as avoiding explicit reward-model fitting.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reward modeling is directly a substrate for RLHF-style preference optimization; DPO is related but intentionally bypasses explicit reward-model training.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Reward-model training, RLHF & RLAIF
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, reward model trained from human comparison data and used in the RLHF pipeline.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reward-model training and RLHF are directly supported, but RLAIF is not covered by the cited packet source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Reflection, critique, verifier & repair loops
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, reward modeling from comparisons; no packet source directly covers reflection, critique, verifier, or repair loops.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited sources do not establish this downstream example.

sentence_reviews.text: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Preference learning, Supervised learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, reward model trained from comparison data; Rafailov et al. DPO paper, preference optimization setup; Murphy PML supervised learning background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Preference/supervised dependencies are supported, but the generic surrogate-objective failure wording is not directly cited.

sentence_reviews.text: Reward modeling uses Preference learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, comparison/preference data used to train a reward model; DPO paper, preference-pair framing.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reward models in RLHF are directly learned from preference/comparison data.

sentence_reviews.text: Reward modeling uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, supervised reward model training from labeled comparisons; Murphy PML supervised learning background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Reward-model fitting from comparison labels is supervised in form; the exact dependency rationale is atlas wording.

sentence_reviews.text: Reward modeling is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Margin, ranking & metric objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Ouyang et al. RLHF paper, reward model trained on ranked/comparison outputs; DPO paper, preference-pair ranking objective context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Ranking/comparison objectives are supported, but the broader margin/metric objective-family label is not directly established.

edge_reviews.notes: RLHF reward modeling directly uses human preferences/comparisons; core strength is supported.
Reward modeling is trained from labeled comparisons; core strength is defensible for standard RLHF reward models.
Ranking/comparison training is supported, but the 'optimizes_for' edge to the broader margin/ranking/metric family is somewhat imprecise.
